paper_title: Phytoplankton Biomass and Species Composition Sampling in the NPSG

methods.description: Collection and processing of ocean color data from MODIS sensor on NASA's Aqua satellite to analyze phytoplankton biomass and species composition.

steps.description: Ocean color data were provided daily from the Moderate Resolution Imaging Spectroradiometer (MODIS) sensor on NASA's Aqua satellite.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch daily ocean color data from MODIS sensor

steps.description: The west coast regional node (WCRN) of NOAA's Coast Watch program processed the NASA data stream and sent it to the ship daily.
steps.searchable_summary: process and transmit MODIS data via NOAA's Coast Watch

steps.description: The data are available at 2.7 km resolution for the Pacific basin, and is mapped to an equal angle grid that is 0.025u latitude by 0.025u longitude using simple arithmetic means to produce composite daily images.
steps.searchable_summary: map data to equal angle grid and produce daily composite images

methods.description: Collection and analysis of seawater samples for nutrient analysis, phytoplankton counts, chlorophyll a, and biogenic silica.

steps.description: Seawater for nutrient analysis, formalin-preserved quantitative phytoplankton counts, epifluorescent symbiont counts, chlorophyll a, and biogenic silica was collected from a Seabird 911 CTD with a 24 Niskin bottle (12-L) rosette.
steps.searchable_summary: collect seawater samples using Seabird 911 CTD

steps.description: Unfiltered seawater was frozen and analyzed ashore on a LaChat Quikchem 8000. Detection limits were 0.05-0.1 mmol L 21 for nitrate+nitrite, silicate and phosphate.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze frozen seawater samples for nutrients

steps.description: 50-mL samples were also analyzed at sea for silicate using a more sensitive colorimetric method.
steps.searchable_summary: perform colorimetric analysis for silicate at sea

methods.description: Methods for visualizing and quantifying phytoplankton using microscopy and fluorescence techniques.

steps.description: Vertical net tows (35 mm mesh) in the upper 50 m were also used to collect phytoplankton for visualization and quantification.
steps.searchable_summary: collect phytoplankton using vertical net tows

steps.description: Net tow samples were examined under transmitted and epifluorescence illumination at sea to confirm the presence of symbionts associated with large diatoms.
steps.searchable_summary: examine net tow samples using fluorescence microscopy
